methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $39.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $14.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

data.narrative:
Figure AI, a Series C-stage AI & Software company founded by Brett Adcock, commands a current post-money valuation of $39.0B following $1B+ in total capital raised. The relevant comp universe consists of 9 private peers (including Skild AI, Wayve, Physical Intelligence, and Black Forest Labs) and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), all operating in frontier AI applications.

Two valuation methods triangulate materially different conclusions. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-subsequent-round advancement to the $39.0B base, implies a forward valuation of $66.3B. Peer-median analysis draws on four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations spanning $3.2B to $14.0B; the median of $7.1B implies substantial compression from current marks. This divergence reflects the outsized scale of Figure AI's capitalization relative to its peer set, where even the highest comparable (Skild AI at $14.0B) sits materially below the current round.

The triangulated range spans $6.0B (low) to $76.2B (high), with a median of $36.7B. The $39.0B current valuation sits near the calculated median, suggesting the last round reflected market consensus. Key uncertainties include the durability of stage-progression multiples in an environment of extended Series C rounds and whether Figure AI's scale justifies premium relative to Skild AI and other architectural peers. Further clarity on revenue traction and path-to-profitability would tighten this band.
